title: HELIX docs backfill: repo
description: Repository-scope HELIX documentation backfill for grctool. Reconstruct canonical artifacts from current evidence, record confidence, and file follow-up work where guidance or execution is still needed.
acceptance:
1. Repo scope is inventoried with explicit coverage ownership or exclusions.
2. Durable backfill report is written under docs/helix/06-iterate/backfill-reports/.
3. High-confidence canonical HELIX artifacts are updated where evidence supports them.
4. Remaining ambiguity and follow-up work are captured in tracker issues or guidance gates.

title: Review CLI and configuration surface
description: Review main entrypoints, Cobra commands, configuration loading, logging bootstrap, and command UX surfaces.
acceptance: 1. main.go, cmd/, configs/, and config-related docs are surveyed.\n2. Current CLI/configuration behavior is summarized with evidence.

title: Review CI, release, and operational delivery surface
description: Review GitHub workflows, Make targets, scripts, release automation, and deployment/operational docs.
acceptance: 1. .github/workflows, scripts/, Makefile, and deployment docs are surveyed.\n2. Delivery constraints and release drift are summarized with evidence.

title: Review evidence, AI, and tool framework
description: Review evidence workflows, tool registry, provider contracts, document generation, and AI-assisted evidence surfaces.
acceptance: 1. internal/tools, internal/services, internal/evidence, internal/templates, internal/formatters, and related docs are surveyed.\n2. Evidence/tool architecture and current capability boundaries are summarized with evidence.

title: Review provider, auth, storage, and sync subsystems
description: Review authentication, provider registry, Tugboat client, storage, scheduler, and sync subsystems for runtime architecture and operational constraints.
acceptance: 1. internal/auth, internal/providers, internal/storage, internal/sync, internal/scheduler, and internal/tugboat are surveyed.\n2. Runtime architecture and operational constraints are summarized with evidence.

title: Consolidate repo backfill findings
description: Consolidate area review findings into a repo-scope backfill report, update high-confidence HELIX artifacts, and create follow-up issues.
acceptance:
1. Coverage ledger is complete.
2. Durable backfill report is written.
3. High-confidence HELIX artifacts are updated.
4. Follow-up issues are created for remaining gaps.

title: Review docs and product surface
description: Review non-HELIX docs plus canonical discover/frame artifacts for current product intent, feature inventory, and terminology drift.
acceptance: 1. docs/, README.md, and canonical discover/frame artifacts are surveyed.\n2. Product behavior and terminology drift are recorded with confidence.

title: Review tests and quality gates
description: Review unit, integration, functional, and e2e tests, VCR fixtures, benchmarks, mutation outputs, and testing docs.
acceptance: 1. test/, test-integration/, internal test surfaces, and testing docs are surveyed.\n2. Current 4-tier test strategy and quality gate drift are summarized with evidence.

title: Align CI and release workflows with Go 1.24 baseline
description: Update GitHub workflow Go versions, test assumptions, and related docs so CI/release automation matches the repository's Go 1.24 baseline declared in go.mod and AGENTS.md.
acceptance: 1. CI and release workflows use a Go version compatible with go.mod.\n2. Related HELIX/testing docs no longer conflict with the automation baseline.\n3. Validation commands are updated if workflow assumptions changed.

title: Normalize HELIX solution design IDs and traceability
description: The current HELIX design stack contains ambiguous solution-design naming, including multiple SD-001 variants. Normalize IDs and cross-phase references so design artifacts have a deterministic traceability scheme.
acceptance: 1. Solution design filenames and identifiers are unambiguous.\n2. Cross-references from frame, design, and build docs resolve cleanly.\n3. Any superseded IDs are documented rather than silently renamed.

title: Clarify roadmap language for system-of-record ambitions
description: Discover/frame artifacts describe GRCTool as evolving into a system of record with broader provider scope, while current implementation remains primarily Tugboat-centric. Clarify which statements are present-day commitments versus longer-term direction.
acceptance: 1. Product vision and PRD distinguish current behavior from roadmap intent.\n2. Feature and design docs use consistent terminology for current versus future scope.\n3. Any guidance-dependent claims are explicitly marked or resolved.
